terms.definition: A rectangular array of numbers arranged in rows and columns, often used to represent linear transformations or structured data like datasets and images.
A square matrix with 1 on the main diagonal and 0 elsewhere; it acts as the multiplicative identity for matrix multiplication of the same size.
An element of a vector space that can be added to other vectors and scaled by numbers while staying in the same space.
An operation that takes an m x n matrix A and an n x p matrix B and produces an m x p matrix C where each entry Ci,j is the dot product of row i of A with column j of B.
The shared size between the number of columns of the left matrix and the number of rows of the right matrix that must match for their product to be defined.
The number of rows of the left matrix and the number of columns of the right matrix, which become the shape of the product matrix.
The computational rule for matrix multiplication where each entry of the product is computed as the dot product of a row of the left matrix with a column of the right matrix.
